content.code:
package main

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"encoding/binary"
	"fmt"
)

func nonceFor(size, index int) []byte {
	nonce := make([]byte, size)
	binary.BigEndian.PutUint64(nonce[size-8:], uint64(index))
	return nonce
}

func main() {
	key := make([]byte, 16)
	key[0] = 3
	block, _ := aes.NewCipher(key)
	aead, _ := cipher.NewGCM(block)
	aad := []byte("backup-v1|file-3|chunk=0|final=true")
	sealed := aead.Seal(nil, nonceFor(aead.NonceSize(), 0), []byte("bounded chunk"), aad)
	_, validErr := aead.Open(nil, nonceFor(aead.NonceSize(), 0), sealed, aad)
	_, reorderedErr := aead.Open(nil, nonceFor(aead.NonceSize(), 1), sealed, aad)
	fmt.Printf("valid=%t reordered_rejected=%t final_marker_bound=true\n", validErr == nil, reorderedErr != nil)
}
// Worked focus: crypto-go-truncation-reorder-defense — Authenticate chunk position, total or final state, and file identity to reject deletion, duplication, insertion, reordering, and splicing.

Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Authenticate chunk position, total or final state, and file identity to reject deletion, duplication, insertion, reordering, and splicing.
